comments: This portrait scored authentic but with moderate confidence — it's one of those images that our system finds genuinely interesting because it has characteristics that could go either way. The freckle distribution across the nose and cheeks scores 0.80 for randomness, which is solidly authentic. The individual hair strands falling across the face show real physics — strand curvature, gravity compliance, varying thickness — with a hair physics compliance score of 0.76. PRNU came back at 0.48, confirming a real sensor. The dark background shows natural light falloff noise rather than a flat synthetic backdrop. Where we hesitate is the skin in the cheek and forehead region — the plasticity index is 0.44, which is above average smoothness but still below the 0.65 synthetic threshold, possibly from post-processing retouching rather than AI generation. Missing EXIF also reduces our hardware confidence anchor.
metadata: No EXIF data — likely stripped during professional post-processing. Natural lens vignetting visible in the dark corners. Shallow depth of field with real optical bokeh characteristics. No AI generation metadata detected.
spectral: No GAN fingerprint. 4 spectral spikes (score 0.31). PRNU 0.48. Freckle randomness 0.80. Hair physics compliance 0.76. Plasticity index 0.44 — elevated but below synthetic threshold. Radial PSD slope -2.28.
